initial_claims.description: The claim() function transfers ETH to msg.sender via .call{value}() before zeroing the balance. An attacker can deploy a contract with a fallback function that re-enters claim() repeatedly, draining all ETH from the contract before balances[msg.sender] is set to 0.
initial_claims.evidence: In Balance.claim(): 'TransferHelper.safeTransferETH(msg.sender, balances[msg.sender])' executes first (which internally does 'to.call{value:value}(new bytes(0))'), then 'balances[msg.sender] = 0' executes after. Exploit: attacker wins a round, gets balance credited via addBalance(), then calls claim(). In fallback, re-enters claim() before balance is zeroed, repeatedly withdrawing the same balance until contract is drained.

There is no reentrancy guard, no checks-effects-interactions pattern, and no other mitigation present in the claim() function. The vulnerable code pattern is exactly as described: ETH transfer occurs before balance zeroing.
